definition: Having given oneself up to vice; immoral; extremely wicked, or sinning without restraint; irreclaimably wicked.
example: Luck abandoned Tom.
exampleZh: 運氣拋棄了湯姆。

definition: In an abnormal manner; in a way that deviates from a standard, norm, or average.
example: Tobias looked abnormally thin.
exampleZh: 託拜厄斯看起來異常瘦弱。

definition: To end a law, system, institution, custom or practice.
example: Slavery was abolished in the nineteenth century.
exampleZh: 奴隸制於十九世紀被廢除。

definition: Of or pertaining to Australian Aboriginal peoples, Aborigines, or their language.
example: Daniel uses traditional aboriginal medicines.
exampleZh: 丹尼爾使用傳統的原住民藥物。

definition: In an abrupt manner; without giving notice, or without the usual forms; suddenly; precipitously.
example: The professor stopped her lecture abruptly when she noticed someone fall off their seat.
exampleZh: 當教授發現有人從座位上摔下來時，她突然停止了演講。

definition: A person who is absent from his or her employment, school, post, duty, etc.
example: The absentee landlord requires a state to enforce his claims to the property.
exampleZh: 缺席房東要求州強制執行他對財產的主張。

definition: Contrary to reason or propriety; obviously and flatly opposed to manifest truth; inconsistent with the plain dictates of common sense; logically contradictory; nonsensical; ridiculous; silly.
example: That sounds absurd.
exampleZh: 這聽起來很荒謬。

definition: Improper treatment or usage; application to a wrong or bad purpose; an unjust, corrupt or wrongful practice or custom.
example: All abuse, whether physical, verbal, psychological or sexual, is bad.
exampleZh: 所有虐待，無論是身體上的、言語上的、心理上的還是性的，都是不好的。

definition: An increase by addition; accession
example: an access of territory
exampleZh: 領土准入

definition: The quality of being accessible, or of admitting approach; receptiveness.
example: The word "accessibility" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「accessibility」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: (usually a mass noun) Lodging in a dwelling or similar living quarters afforded to travellers in hotels or on cruise ships, or prisoners, etc.
example: They live in temporary accommodation.
exampleZh: 他們住在臨時住處。

definition: (manner) Agreeably; correspondingly; suitably
example: Plan your life accordingly.
exampleZh: 相應地規劃你的生活。

definition: To heap up in a mass; to pile up; to collect or bring together (either literally or figuratively)
example: He wishes to accumulate a sum of money.
exampleZh: 他希望累積一筆錢。

definition: The act of accusing.
example: The accusation was false.
exampleZh: 該指控是錯誤的。

definition: To suffer pain; to be the source of, or be in, pain, especially continued dull pain; to be distressed.
example: My muscles ache.
exampleZh: 我的肌肉疼痛。

definition: Capable of being achieved, which either means possible or probable.
example: The word "achievable" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「可實現」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A sour substance.
example: Acid corrodes metal.
exampleZh: 酸腐蝕金屬。

definition: The act or process of acquiring.
example: The acquisition of sports equipment can be fun in itself.
exampleZh: 購買運動器材本身就很有趣。

definition: In an active manner.
example: He was actively listening.
exampleZh: 他正在積極傾聽。

definition: Existing in reality, not just potentially; really acted or acting; occurring in fact.
example: The actual government expenses dramatically exceed the budget.
exampleZh: 政府的實際支出大大超出了預算。

definition: Brief, quick, short.
example: It was an acute event.
exampleZh: 這是一次嚴重的事件。

definition: Capable of adapting or of being adapted.
example: I am adaptable.
exampleZh: 我適應力強。

definition: A person who is addicted, especially to a harmful drug
example: He is an addict when it comes to chocolate cookies.
exampleZh: 他對巧克力餅乾上癮。

definition: Having a biochemical addiction.
example: Barih became addicted.
exampleZh: 巴里上癮了。

definition: A state that is characterized by compulsive drug use or compulsive engagement in rewarding behavior, despite negative consequences.
example: The word "addiction" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「成癮」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: By way of addition; in addition to; also.
example: The word "additionally" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「另外」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: Equal to or fulfilling some requirement.
example: an adequate definition
exampleZh: 充分的定義

definition: In an adequate manner.
example: Uranus hasn't been adequately probed.
exampleZh: 天王星尚未得到充分探測。

definition: The settling or balancing of a financial account
example: The credit card company made an adjustment to my account to waive the late fee.
exampleZh: 信用卡公司對我的帳戶進行了調整，免除了滯納金。

definition: Of or relating to administering or administration.
example: I hate administrative work.
exampleZh: 我討厭行政工作。

definition: Deserving of the highest esteem or admiration.
example: It's admirable that Shelley overcame her handicap and excelled in her work.
exampleZh: 雪萊克服了自己的障礙並在工作中表現出色，令人欽佩。

definition: In an admiring manner.
example: The word "admiringly" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「admiringly」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: As is acknowledged to be true; by general admission; confessedly.
example: Admittedly, vandalism is a bit of a problem.
exampleZh: 誠然，破壞公物是一個問題。

definition: To take or receive as one's own what is not so naturally.
example: He adopted a new look in order to fit in with his new workmates.
exampleZh: 為了適應新同事，他換了新的面貌。

definition: The state of being adopted; the acceptance of a child of other parents as if he or she were one's own child.
example: A Chinese baby girl was given away for adoption.
exampleZh: 一名中國女嬰被送人收養。

definition: To love with one's entire heart and soul; regard with deep respect and affection.
example: It is obvious to everyone that Gerry adores Heather.
exampleZh: 大家都知道，格里很喜歡希瑟。

definition: To make more beautiful and attractive; to decorate.
example: a character adorned with every Christian grace
exampleZh: 一個充滿基督教恩典的人物

definition: A forward move; improvement or progression.
example: an advance in health or knowledge
exampleZh: 健康或知識的進步

definition: The act of advancing, ; promotion to a higher place or dignity
example: the advancement of learning
exampleZh: 學習的進步

definition: Arrival; onset; a time when something first comes or appears
example: A chocolate is hidden behind each door of this Advent calendar.
exampleZh: 這款降臨節日曆的每扇門後面都藏著一塊巧克力。

definition: (of a person) Inclined to adventure; willing to incur risks; prone to embark in hazardous enterprise; rashly daring.
example: Tom seemed adventurous.
exampleZh: 湯姆似乎很喜歡冒險。

definition: Unfavorable; antagonistic in purpose or effect; hostile; actively opposing one's interests or wishes; contrary to one's welfare; acting against; working in an opposing direction.
example: adverse criticism
exampleZh: 不利的批評

definition: In an adverse manner.
example: The word "adversely" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「adversely」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: The state of adverse conditions; state of misfortune or calamity.
example: Adversity strengthens the foundations.
exampleZh: 逆境可以夯實基礎。

definition: (of a person) Having affection or warm regard; loving; fond.
example: She eulogised her always warm and affectionate brother.
exampleZh: 她稱讚她總是溫暖而深情的哥哥。

definition: In an affectionate manner.
example: He looks at her affectionately.
exampleZh: 他深情地看著她。

definition: Able to be afforded; inexpensive or reasonably priced.
example: an affordable vacation in the local area
exampleZh: 在當地度過一個經濟實惠的假期

definition: (relative to past) Having existed for so great a period of time that its longevity cannot be expressed.
example: The ageless pyramids stood prominently against the sunset.
exampleZh: 永恆的金字塔在夕陽的襯托下格外顯眼。

definition: In an aggressive manner.
example: She behaves aggressively.
exampleZh: 她的行為具有攻擊性。

definition: Extreme pain.
example: Rodrigo screamed in agony.
exampleZh: 羅德里戈痛苦地尖叫。

definition: To point or direct a missile, or a weapon which propels as missile, towards an object or spot with the intent of hitting it
example: He aimed at the target, but the arrow flew straight over it.
exampleZh: 他瞄準了目標，但箭卻筆直地飛過目標。

definition: A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift
example: Algeria has sophisticated fighter aircraft.
exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞擁有先進的戰鬥機。

definition: A group of two or more trained individuals, formed as a team that operates an aircraft.
example: The aircrew ran to the bomber.
exampleZh: 機組人員跑向轟炸機。

definition: A person addicted to alcohol.
example: The word "alcoholic" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「酒精」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A chronic disease caused by compulsive and uncontrollable consumption of alcoholic beverages, leading to addiction and deterioration in health and social functioning.
example: The word "alcoholism" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「酗酒」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A state of readiness for potential combat.
example: an airborne alert; ground alert
exampleZh: 空中警報；地面警報

definition: An assertion, especially an accusation, not necessarily based on facts.
example: She put forth several allegations regarding her partner in hopes of discrediting his actions.
exampleZh: 她對她的伴侶提出了幾項指控，希望抹黑他的行為。

definition: The state of being allied.
example: matrimonial alliances; an alliance between church and state, or between two countries
exampleZh: 婚姻聯盟；教會與國家或兩國之間的聯盟

definition: A person, group, or state (etc) which is associated with another for a common cause; one united to another by treaty or common purpose; a confederate.
example: the two countries were allies in WWI
exampleZh: 兩國在第一次世界大戰中是盟友

definition: Together with or at the same time.
example: The nurse worked alongside the doctor.
exampleZh: 護士與醫生一起工作。

definition: According to the sequence of the letters of the alphabet.
example: All names were placed into an alphabetical list.
exampleZh: 所有名字都按字母順序排列在列表中。

definition: A table or similar flat-topped structure used for religious rites.
example: The altar of media believers is television.
exampleZh: 媒體信徒的祭壇是電視。

definition: To change the form or structure of.
example: Nothing can alter the past.
exampleZh: 沒有什麼可以改變過去。

definition: To happen, succeed, or act by turns; to follow reciprocally in place or time; followed by with.
example: The flood and ebb tides alternate with each other.
exampleZh: 漲潮和落潮交替出現。

definition: In an alternative way
example: The word "alternatively" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「替代」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: The absolute height of a location, usually measured from sea level.
example: As the altitude increases, the temperature gets lower, so remember to bring warm clothes to the mountains.
exampleZh: 隨著海拔的升高，氣溫逐漸降低，所以上山記得帶保暖的衣服。

definition: To fill with wonder and surprise; to astonish, astound, surprise or perplex.
example: He was amazed when he found that the girl was a robot.
exampleZh: 當他發現這個女孩是機器人時，他感到很驚訝。

definition: The condition of being amazed; overwhelming wonder, as from surprise, sudden fear, horror, or admiration; astonishment.
example: The word "amazement" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「amazement」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: In an amazing manner; in a way that causes amazement; wonderfully.
example: That violin solo was played amazingly.
exampleZh: 那段小提琴獨奏演奏得令人驚艷。

definition: A corporate representative, often the public face of the company.
example: As front hall porter, you are an ambassador for the hotel.
exampleZh: 身為前廳行李員，您是飯店的大使。

definition: Something, particularly words and sentences, that is open to more than one interpretation, explanation or meaning, if that meaning etc cannot be determined from its context.
example: His speech was made with such great ambiguity that neither supporter nor opponent could be certain of his true position.
exampleZh: 他的演講非常含糊，無論是支持者還是反對者都無法確定他的真實立場。

definition: Open to multiple interpretations.
example: The politician was criticized for his ambiguous statements and lack of precision.
exampleZh: 這位政客因其言論含糊和缺乏精確性而受到批評。

definition: A gaseous compound of hydrogen and nitrogen, NH3, with a pungent smell and taste.
example: The ammonia molecule is composed of four atoms.
exampleZh: 氨分子由四個原子組成。

definition: Denotes a mingling or intermixing with distinct or separable objects. See usage note at amidst.
example: Janek feels very at ease amongst his friends, but gets incredibly nervous when meeting new people.
exampleZh: 賈內克在朋友中感到非常自在，但在結識新朋友時卻變得異常緊張。

definition: Large; great in size, extent, capacity, or bulk; for example spacious, roomy or widely extended.
example: an ample house
exampleZh: 寬敞的房子

definition: Anything that amplifies, or makes something larger or more intense.
example: The guitar and amplifier are sold separately.
exampleZh: 吉他和擴音器單獨販售。

definition: To render larger, more extended, or more intense.
example: amplify a microscopes
exampleZh: 放大顯微鏡

definition: To entertain or occupy in a pleasant manner; to stir with pleasing emotions.
example: I watch these movies because they amuse me.
exampleZh: 我看這些電影是因為它們讓我開心。

definition: A relationship of resemblance or equivalence between two situations, people, or objects, especially when used as a basis for explanation or extrapolation.
example: The analogy is essentially correct.
exampleZh: 這個類比基本上是正確的。

definition: Someone who analyzes.
example: Yanni is an Algerian financial analyst.
exampleZh: 雅尼是阿爾及利亞的金融分析師。

definition: A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.
example: Gabriel heard an anchor dropping.
exampleZh: 加百列聽到錨落下的聲音。

definition: That into which something is anchored or fastened.
example: the anchorages of the Brooklyn Bridge
exampleZh: 布魯克林大橋的錨地

definition: The main host of a television or radio program, particularly one relating to the broadcast of news.
example: The anchorman had an audience with the king.
exampleZh: 主持人覲見國王。

definition: The primary reporter on a television news broadcast.
example: The word "anchorperson" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「錨人」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: Extreme pain, either of body or mind; excruciating distress.
example: Yanni could clearly hear the anguish in Rima's voice.
exampleZh: 雅尼清楚地聽出了麗瑪聲音中的痛苦。

definition: To impart motion or the appearance of motion to.
example: If we animate the model, we can see the complexity of the action.
exampleZh: 如果我們為模型製作動畫，我們可以看到動作的複雜性。

definition: Without any name acknowledged of a person responsible
example: an anonymous pamphlet
exampleZh: 匿名小冊子

definition: Intended to attack aircraft.
example: The word "antiaircraft" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「antiaircraft」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: (pharmaceutical effect) Killing or inhibiting bacteria.
example: This drug has an antibacterial effect.
exampleZh: 該藥具有抗菌作用。

definition: That is used to treat cancer.
example: The word "anticancer" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「抗癌」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: To take up or introduce (something) prematurely.
example: The advocate plans to anticipate a part of her argument.
exampleZh: 辯護人計劃預測她的論點的一部分。

definition: The act of anticipating, taking up, placing, or considering something beforehand, or before the proper time in natural order.
example: Often the anticipation of a shot is worse than the pain of the stick.
exampleZh: 通常，射擊的預期比棍子的疼痛更糟糕。

definition: Playful, funny, absurd
example: The word "antique" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「古董」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: Preventing the buildup of static electricity.
example: The word "antistatic" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「抗靜電」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A piece of software that is used to detect, delete and or neutralize computer-based viruses.
example: Bill Gates wrote viruses and sold antivirus software.
exampleZh: 比爾蓋茲編寫病毒並銷售防毒軟體。

definition: The text character ’, which serves as a punctuation mark in various languages and as a diacritical mark in certain rare contexts.
example: I changed the apostrophe to the straight one that we use.
exampleZh: 我將撇號更改為我們使用的直撇號。

definition: To call on (someone) for aid
example: I appeal to all of you to help the orphans.
exampleZh: 我呼籲大家幫助孤兒。

definition: One who applies for something; one who makes a request; a petitioner.
example: Our job advertisement attracted seven applicants.
exampleZh: 我們的招募廣告吸引了七位應徵者。

definition: Showing appreciation or gratitude.
example: Tom was appreciative.
exampleZh: 湯姆很感激。

definition: To draw near, in a figurative sense; to make advances; to approximate.
example: as he approaches to the character of the ablest statesman.
exampleZh: 當他接近最有能力的政治家的性格。

definition: In an appropriate manner; properly; suitably.
example: Start behaving appropriately.
exampleZh: 開始表現得體。

definition: Nearing correctness; nearly exact; not perfectly accurate.
example: NASA's Genesis spacecraft has on board an ion monitor to record the speed, density, temperature and approximate composition of the solar wind ions.
exampleZh: 美國太空總署的創世紀太空船上裝有離子監視器，用於記錄太陽風離子的速度、密度、溫度和大致成分。

definition: Any place covered by an arch; an archway.
example: to pass into the arch of a bridge
exampleZh: 進入橋拱

definition: (now only in compounds) Pertaining to the celestial north pole, or to the pole star.
example: She has finally reached the Arctic.
exampleZh: 她終於到達了北極。

definition: An enclosed area, often outdoor, for the presentation of sporting events (sports arena) or other spectacular events; earthen area, often oval, specifically for rodeos (North America) or circular area for bullfights (especially Hispanic America).
example: A large crowd filled the seats of the arena.
exampleZh: 競技場的座位上擠滿了一大群人。

definition: The nobility, or the hereditary ruling class.
example: I did everything possible to appear as a member of the aristocracy.
exampleZh: 我千方百計地以貴族的身份出現。

definition: The amount an arm or arms can hold.
example: The word "armful" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「armful」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: To stimulate feelings.
example: The new building proposals in the village are arousing unneeded discomfort.
exampleZh: 村裡的新建築提案引起了不必要的不​​適。

definition: Having excessive pride in oneself, often with contempt or disrespect for others.
example: Americans are arrogant.
exampleZh: 美國人很傲慢。

definition: An efferent blood vessel from the heart, conveying blood away from the heart regardless of oxygenation status; see pulmonary artery.
example: I've got a bad heart: coronary artery disease.
exampleZh: 我的心臟不好：冠狀動脈疾病。

definition: An animal of the subkingdom Articulata.
example: Tom is pretty articulate.
exampleZh: 湯姆非常善於表達。

definition: In an artificial manner.
example: The actor spoke his lines artificially, with too much intonation.
exampleZh: 演員說台詞時很做作，語調太多。

definition: Significant artistic skill.
example: Their fast talking is a smokescreen for their con artistry.
exampleZh: 他們的語速很快就只是他們騙術的煙幕彈。

definition: The solid remains of a fire.
example: Ash from a fireplace can restore minerals to your garden's soil.
exampleZh: 壁爐中的灰燼可以將礦物質還原到花園的土壤中。

definition: The act of aspiring or ardently desiring; an ardent wish or desire, chiefly after what is elevated or spiritual (with common adjunct adpositions being to and of).
example: Morgan has an aspiration of winning the game.
exampleZh: 摩根渴望贏得這場比賽。

definition: To have a strong desire or ambition to achieve something.
example: He aspires to become a successful doctor.
exampleZh: 他渴望成為一名成功的醫生。

definition: A violent onset or attack with physical means, for example blows, weapons, etc.
example: to make assault upon a man, a house, or a town
exampleZh: 攻擊一個人、一棟房屋或一座城鎮

definition: To put together.
example: He assembled the model ship.
exampleZh: 他組裝了模型船。

definition: A set of pieces that work together in unison as a mechanism or device.
example: In order to change the bearing, you must first remove the gearbox assembly.
exampleZh: 為了更換軸承，必須先拆下變速箱總成。

definition: To declare with assurance or plainly and strongly; to state positively.
example: He would often assert that there was life on other planets.
exampleZh: 他經常斷言其他行星上有生命。

definition: To determine, estimate or judge the value of; to evaluate
example: He assessed the situation.
exampleZh: 他評估了形勢。

definition: The act of assessing or an amount (of tax, levy or duty etc) assessed.
example: I disagree with your assessment.
exampleZh: 我不同意你的評價。

definition: The act of assuming, or taking to or upon oneself; the act of taking up or adopting.
example: His assumption of secretarial duties was timely.
exampleZh: 他擔任秘書職務是及時的。

definition: Excess of boldness; impudence; audacity
example: his assurance is intolerable
exampleZh: 他的保證令人難以忍受

definition: (followed by that or of) To give (someone) confidence in the trustworthiness of (something).
example: He assured of his commitment to her happiness.
exampleZh: 他向她保證他會致力於讓她幸福。

definition: To surprise greatly.
example: The word "astonish" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「驚訝」一詞通常用在正式英語中。

definition: Amazed; surprised.
example: Everyone is astonished.
exampleZh: 所有人都驚愕不已。

definition: Causing astonishment.
example: They're astonishing.
exampleZh: 他們太令人驚訝了。

definition: The feeling or experience of being astonished; great surprise.
example: The class looked on in astonishment as their teacher proceeded to tear the pages out of the textbook.
exampleZh: 當老師開始把課本上的書頁撕下來時，全班都驚訝地看著。

definition: The study of the physical universe beyond the Earth's atmosphere, including the process of mapping locations and properties of the matter and radiation in the universe.
example: Martino was interested in astronomy.
exampleZh: 馬蒂諾對天文學很有興趣。

definition: (especially Britain) A group of sporting activities including track and field, road running, cross country and racewalking.
example: The word "athletics" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「田徑」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: Fond of (used with to).
example: I'm very attached to my pets.
exampleZh: 我非常依戀我的寵物。

definition: Able to be accomplished, achieved, or obtained.
example: Peace in Libya is attainable.
exampleZh: 利比亞的和平是可以實現的。

definition: The act of attaining; the act of arriving at or reaching; the act of obtaining by exertion or effort.
example: The word "attainment" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: “attain”一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: The state of attending; presence or waiting upon.
example: Attendance at the meeting is required.
exampleZh: 必須出席會議。

definition: Paying attention; noticing, watching, listening, or attending closely.
example: She is an attentive listener, but does not like to talk much.
exampleZh: 她是個細心的傾聽者，但不喜歡多說話。

definition: In an attentive manner.
example: The word "attentively" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「注意」一詞通常用於正式英語。

definition: The state of being attractive or engaging.
example: The word "attractiveness" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「吸引力」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A characteristic or quality of a thing.
example: His finest attribute is his kindness.
exampleZh: 他最好的品質就是他的善良。

definition: Not conforming to the normal type.
example: Hutchinson's freckle is a lentigo composed of atypical melanocytes.
exampleZh: 哈欽森雀斑是一種由非典型黑色素細胞組成的雀斑。

definition: An Asian plant, Solanum melongena, cultivated for its edible purple, green, or white ovoid fruit
example: Aubergine and cheese makes a great vegetarian meal.
exampleZh: 茄子和起司是一頓美味的素食餐。

definition: An independent review and examination of records and activities to assess the adequacy of system controls, to ensure compliance with established policies and operational procedures, and to recommend necessary changes in controls, policies, or procedures
example: National Assembly audit
exampleZh: 國會審計

definition: A performance, by an aspiring performer, to demonstrate suitability or talent.
example: I've been to five auditions this week.
exampleZh: 這週我參加了五次試鏡。

definition: Arising or originating from a figure of authority
example: The authoritative rules in this school come not from the headmaster but from the aged matron.
exampleZh: 這所學校的權威規則不是來自校長，而是來自年邁的女校長。

definition: The quality of being available.
example: What is your availability this week?
exampleZh: 這週你有空嗎？

definition: To wait for.
example: I await your reply to my letter.
exampleZh: 我等待你對我的信的答覆。

definition: A feeling of fear and reverence.
example: Gabriel observed Rima with much awe.
exampleZh: 加百列滿懷敬畏地觀察著莉瑪。

definition: An imaginary line around which an object spins (an axis of rotation) or is symmetrically arranged (an axis of symmetry).
example: The Earth rotates once a day on its axis
exampleZh: 地球每天繞地軸自轉一周

definition: A reserve or substitute.
example: If the goalkeeper is injured, we have a backup.
exampleZh: 如果守門員受傷了，我們還有替補。

definition: A yard to the rear of a house or similar residence.
example: Sami decorated the backyard.
exampleZh: 薩米裝飾了後院。

definition: A distinctive mark, token, sign, emblem or cognizance, worn on one's clothing, as an insignia of some rank, or of the membership of an organization.
example: the badge of a society; the badge of a policeman
exampleZh: 社團的徽章；警察的徽章

definition: Of or pertaining to bad temper; showing anger easily or customarily.
example: His bad-tempered outburst caught his friends by surprise.
exampleZh: 他的暴躁脾氣讓他的朋友們大吃一驚。

definition: To hold (an object or objects) precariously; to support on a narrow base, so as to keep from falling.
example: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.
exampleZh: 我把咖啡杯放在膝蓋上。

definition: Containing elements in appropriate proportion; proportionately weighted on all dimensions and therefore unlikely to tip over.
example: He believed he rarely got sick because of his balanced diet.
exampleZh: 他相信，由於飲食均衡，他很少生病。

definition: Refusing to proceed or cooperate.
example: a balky horse
exampleZh: 一匹頑固的馬

definition: A kind of narrative poem, adapted for recitation or singing; especially, a sentimental or romantic poem in short stanzas.
example: The poet composed a ballad praising the heroic exploits of the fallen commander.
exampleZh: 這位詩人創作了一首歌謠，歌頌陣亡將領的英勇事蹟。

definition: A classical form of dance.
example: Rima has ballet practice.
exampleZh: 裡瑪有芭蕾舞練習。

definition: Originally, a small ball placed in a container to cast a vote; now, by extension, a piece of paper or card used for this purpose, or some other means used to signify a vote.
example: You can vote by absentee ballot.
exampleZh: 您可以透過缺席選票進行投票。

definition: A public proclamation or edict; a summons by public proclamation. Chiefly, in early use, a summons to arms.
example: Bans is common and ordinary amongst the Feudists, and signifies a proclamation, or any public notice.
exampleZh: 禁令在封建主義者中是常見且普通的，表示公告或任何公告。

definition: One who conducts the business of banking; one who, individually, or as a member of a company, keeps an establishment for the deposit or loan of money, or for traffic in money, bills of exchange, etc.
example: Is your banker honest?
exampleZh: 你的銀行家誠實嗎？

definition: To force into bankruptcy.
example: She's completely bankrupt.
exampleZh: 她徹底破產了。

definition: A legally declared or recognized condition of insolvency of a person or organization.
example: The corporation filed for bankruptcy.
exampleZh: 該公司申請破產。

definition: The place of business of a barber; a store where a person (usually a man) can go to get a haircut.
example: The barbershop is opposite the bank.
exampleZh: 理髮店在銀行對面。

definition: (‘the bare’) The surface, the (bare) skin.
example: The word "bare" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「bare」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: To make a bargain; to make a deal or contract for the exchange of property or services; to negotiate
example: They had to bargain for a few minutes to get a decent price for the rug.
exampleZh: 他們不得不討價還價幾分鐘才能為地毯拿到一個合適的價格。

definition: To make a short, loud, explosive noise with the vocal organs (said of animals, especially dogs).
example: The neighbour's dog is always barking.
exampleZh: 鄰居家的狗總是狂吠。

definition: A cereal of the species Hordeum vulgare, or its grains, often used as food or to make beer and other malted drinks.
example: He harvested barley and wheat.
exampleZh: 他收穫了大麥和小麥。

definition: An arena.
example: Maple Leaf Gardens was a grand old barn.
exampleZh: 楓葉花園是一座宏偉的舊穀倉。

definition: An instrument for measuring atmospheric pressure.
example: The retail price index is a barometer of economic activity.
exampleZh: 零售物價指數是經濟活動的晴雨表。

definition: Unable to bear children; sterile.
example: I silently wept as my daughter's husband rejected her. What would she do now that she was no longer a maiden but also barren?
exampleZh: 當我女兒的丈夫拒絕她時，我默默地哭泣。現在她不再是處女，而且還不能生育，她該怎麼辦？

definition: A structure that bars passage.
example: The language barrier proved insurmountable.
exampleZh: 事實證明，語言障礙是無法克服的。

definition: One who tends a bar or pub; a person preparing and serving drinks at a bar.
example: Sami was the bartender.
exampleZh: 薩米是調酒師。

definition: A building with baths for communal use.
example: Luckily for Neo, there is a weightlifting set inside the bathhouse.
exampleZh: 對尼奧來說幸運的是，浴室裡有一個舉重器材。

definition: The area where a land battle is or was fought, which is not necessarily a field.
example: 1886 The night of the 16th of May found McPherson's command bivouacked from two to six miles west of the battlefield, along the line of the road to Vicksburg — Ulysses S. Grant, Personal memoirs of U.S. Grant, Chapter 35.
exampleZh: 1886 年 5 月 16 日晚上，麥克弗森的指揮部在距離戰場以西 2 到 6 英里的地方紮營，沿著通往維克斯堡的公路——尤利西斯·S·格蘭特，美國格蘭特的個人回憶錄，第 35 章。

definition: A small short-legged smooth-coated scenthound, often tricolored and sometimes used for hunting hares. Its friendly disposition makes it suitable as a family pet.
example: Yet I get no repose and I can't even doze without dreaming I'm back on the Beagle.
exampleZh: 然而我卻得不到休息，我甚至無法打瞌睡而不夢見自己回到了小獵犬號上。

definition: Anatomical uses.
example: What bird has the longest beak?
exampleZh: 什麼鳥的喙最長？

definition: The maximum width of a vessel (note that a vessel with a beam of 15 foot can also be said to be 15 foot abeam)
example: This ship has more beam than that one.
exampleZh: 這艘船的橫樑比那艘多。

definition: To endeavour to depress the price of, or prices in.
example: to bear a railroad stock
exampleZh: 持有鐵路股票

definition: A pulsation or throb.
example: a beat of the heart; the beat of the pulse
exampleZh: 心跳；脈搏的跳動

definition: To make beautiful, or to increase the beauty of.
example: Hundreds of years ago, married Japanese women would blacken their teeth to beautify themselves.
exampleZh: 數百年前，已婚的日本女性會把牙齒塗黑以美化自己。

definition: To make a loud, deep, hollow noise like the roar of an angry bull.
example: Do the oxen bellow when their mangers are full?
exampleZh: 當牛的馬槽滿了時，它們會吼叫嗎？

definition: The feeling that one belongs.
example: A need for belonging seems fundamental to humans.
exampleZh: 歸屬感的需求似乎是人類的基本需求。

definition: A curve.
example: There's a sharp bend in the road ahead.
exampleZh: 前面的路有一個急轉彎。

definition: Helpful or good to something or someone.
example: Recycling and reusing garbage can be beneficial to the environment.
exampleZh: 回收和再利用垃圾可能對環境有益。

definition: To be or to provide a benefit to.
example: This benefit is invaluable.
exampleZh: 這個好處是無價的。

definition: Disposition to do good.
example: A certain hallmark of wisdom is the cheerful benevolence it confers.
exampleZh: 智慧的一個標誌是它所帶來的令人愉快的仁慈。

definition: Having a disposition to do good.
example: Chinese and Eastern mythologies describe dragons as benevolent.
exampleZh: 中國和東方神話將龍描述為仁慈的。

definition: A small succulent fruit, of any one of many varieties.
example: This berry is tasty.
exampleZh: 這漿果很好吃。

definition: To give; confer; impart gratuitously; present something to someone or something, especially as a gift or honour.
example: Medals were bestowed on the winning team.
exampleZh: 獲勝隊被授予獎牌。

definition: A book or other product that has sold in large numbers.
example: Her book became an international bestseller.
exampleZh: 她的書成為國際暢銷書。

definition: To deliver into the hands of an enemy by treachery or fraud, in violation of trust; to give up treacherously or faithlessly.
example: an officer betrayed the city
exampleZh: 一名軍官背叛了這座城市

definition: To confuse, disorientate, or puzzle someone, especially with many different choices.
example: All the different possible options may bewilder us.
exampleZh: 所有不同的可能選擇可能會讓我們感到困惑。

definition: The state of being bewildered.
example: Tom's face clearly showed bewilderment coupled with irritation.
exampleZh: 湯姆的臉上明顯表現出困惑和惱怒。

definition: To issue a command; to tell.
example: He bade me come in.
exampleZh: 他請我進來。

definition: That which one is bidden to do; a command.
example: Tom stopped bidding.
exampleZh: 湯姆停止出價。

definition: A brief two-piece bathing suit worn by women, especially one that exposes the midriff and navel.
example: Rima was walking around in a bikini.
exampleZh: 裡瑪穿著比基尼四處走動。

definition: An unspecified very large number.
example: There were billions of people at the concert.
exampleZh: 音樂會現場有數十億人。

definition: To cohere or stick together in a mass.
example: Just to make the cheese more binding
exampleZh: 只是為了讓起司更有黏合力

definition: Biographical sketch
example: If you use someone else's drawing as your profile picture, make sure to credit the artist in your bio.
exampleZh: 如果您使用其他人的畫作作為您的個人資料圖片，請務必在您的個人簡介中註明該藝術家。

definition: Of or relating to biology.
example: The word "biological" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「生物學」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: The use of living organisms (especially microorganisms) in industrial, agricultural, medical and other technological applications.
example: Biotechnology will bring about a revolution in agriculture.
exampleZh: 生物技術將帶來農業革命。

definition: A cage to keep pet or zoological specimen birds in.
example: There's a parrot in the birdcage.
exampleZh: 鳥籠裡有一隻鸚鵡。

definition: The location where a person was born.
example: Portsmouth was Charles Dickens’ birthplace.
exampleZh: 朴茨茅斯是查爾斯·狄更斯的出生地。

definition: In a bitter manner.
example: They argued bitterly.
exampleZh: 他們爭論得很激烈。

definition: Strangely unconventional in style or appearance.
example: Bizarre things started happening.
exampleZh: 奇怪的事情開始發生。

definition: The sharp cutting edge of a knife, chisel, or other tool, a razor blade/sword.
example: This blade can slay immortals.
exampleZh: 此劍可斬仙。

definition: The continuous blowing to which one charge of ore or metal is subjected in a furnace
example: many tons of iron were melted at a blast
exampleZh: 許多噸鐵一下子就被熔化了

definition: Intense, direct light accompanied with heat.
example: to seek shelter from the blaze of the sun
exampleZh: 尋求庇護以躲避烈日的炙烤

definition: A mixture of two or more things.
example: Our department has a good blend of experienced workers and young promise.
exampleZh: 我們部門擁有經驗豐富的員工和年輕有為的員工。

definition: In a blind manner; without sight; sightlessly.
example: I stumbled blindly through the forest, branches whipping at my face.
exampleZh: 我盲目地跌跌撞撞地穿過森林，樹枝拍打著我的臉。

definition: The condition of being blind; unable to see.
example: The word "blindness" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「blindness」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: To close and reopen both eyes quickly.
example: The loser in the staring game is the person who blinks first.
exampleZh: 凝視遊戲中的失敗者是先眨眼的人。

definition: The flow of blood through the circulatory system of an animal
example: The vaccine is not injected into the bloodstream, but into the muscle.
exampleZh: 疫苗不是注射到血液中，而是注射到肌肉中。

definition: Covered in blood.
example: All that remained of his right hand after the accident was a bloody stump.
exampleZh: 事故發生後，他的右手只剩下血淋淋的殘肢。

definition: Opening in blossoms; flowering.
example: The flowers were blooming.
exampleZh: 花朵盛開。

definition: A flower, especially one indicating that a fruit tree is fruiting; a mass of such flowers.
example: The blossom has come early this year.
exampleZh: 今年花開得早。

definition: To make indistinct or hazy, to obscure or dim.
example: to blur a photograph by moving the camera while taking it
exampleZh: 透過在拍攝時移動相機來模糊照片

definition: An abrupt outburst.
example: Please think about your reply and don't just blurt out the first thing that comes to mind.
exampleZh: 請考慮一下你的回答，不要只是脫口而出你想到的第一件事。

definition: To become red in the face (and sometimes experience an associated feeling of warmth), especially due to shyness, shame, excitement, or embarrassment.
example: He wasn't used to this much attention, so he blushed as he saw dozens of pairs of eyes watching him.
exampleZh: 他不太習慣這麼多的關注，看到幾十雙眼睛注視著他，他臉紅了。

definition: That boils or boil.
example: boiling kettle  boiling oil
exampleZh: 燒水壺  燒油

definition: A dwelling; habitation; building.
example: Fortune favours the bold.
exampleZh: 命運眷顧勇敢的人。

definition: A (usually) metal fastener consisting of a cylindrical body that is threaded, with a larger head on one end. It can be inserted into an unthreaded hole up to the head, with a nut then threaded on the other end; a heavy machine screw.
example: The problem's solution struck him like a bolt from the blue.
exampleZh: 問題的解決對他來說猶如晴天霹靂。

definition: A medieval primitive cannon, used chiefly in sieges for throwing heavy stone balls.
example: The word "bombard" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: “bombard”一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A military aircraft designed to carry and drop bombs.
example: My grandfather got seriously injured by a bomber during the war.
exampleZh: 我的祖父在戰爭期間被轟炸機嚴重炸傷。

definition: The action of dropping bombs from the air.
example: I was bombing down the road on my motorbike.
exampleZh: 我騎著摩托車在路上狂奔。

definition: Resembling, having the appearance or consistence of, or relating to bone; osseous.
example: Look at him! He's bony, he can barely pick up a bottle of water.
exampleZh: 看看他！他骨瘦如柴，幾乎連一瓶水都拿不起來。

definition: Fond of reading or studying, especially said of someone lacking social skills as a result.
example: She's undergone a metamorphosis from a quiet, bookish girl to an outspoken feminist activist.
exampleZh: 她經歷了從一個安靜、書生氣的女孩到一個直言不諱的女權運動家的蛻變。

definition: A small or thin book.
example: The complete booklet is available for free online.
exampleZh: 完整的小冊子可在線上免費取得。

definition: A strip of material used to mark a place in a book.
example: You dropped a bookmark.
exampleZh: 你掉了一個書籤。

definition: A push from behind, as to one who is endeavoring to climb.
example: The controversy gave a boost to the author's sales.
exampleZh: 這場爭議促進了作者的銷售。

definition: A small stall for the display and sale of goods.
example: Where's the nearest phone booth?
exampleZh: 最近的電話亭在哪裡？

definition: Tending to give orders to others, especially when unwarranted; domineering.
example: Tom became bossy.
exampleZh: 湯姆變得專橫。

definition: The scientific study of plants, a branch of biology. Typically those disciplines that involve the whole plant.
example: the botany of Greenland
exampleZh: 格陵蘭島植物學

definition: Caused to show discomposure.
example: Would it bother you if I smoked?
exampleZh: 如果我抽煙你會介意嗎？

definition: Causing bother or perplexity.
example: Do you think that bothersome things disappear if you ignore them?
exampleZh: 你認為如果你忽略那些煩人的事情，它們就會消失嗎？

definition: To seal (a liquid) into a bottle for later consumption. Also fig.
example: This plant bottles vast quantities of spring water every day.
exampleZh: 這家工廠每天都會生產大量的泉水。

definition: A change of direction of motion after hitting the ground or an obstacle.
example: Them pro-ballers got bounce!
exampleZh: 他們的職業球員得到了反彈！

definition: A whiskey distilled from a mixture of grains in which at least 51% is corn, aged in charred, new oak barrels. Made in the United States.
example: Mary poured herself another shot of bourbon.
exampleZh: 瑪莉又為自己倒了一杯波本威士忌。

definition: To play music on (a stringed) instrument using a bow.
example: The musician bowed his violin expertly.
exampleZh: 音樂家熟練地拉著小提琴。

definition: A participant (fighter) in a boxing match.
example: You can tell she's a boxer by looking at her nose.
exampleZh: 看她的鼻子就知道她是拳擊手。

definition: A fixture attached to a wall to hold up a shelf.
example: tax bracket, age bracket
exampleZh: 稅級、年齡級

definition: A boast or boasting; bragging; ostentatious pretence or self-glorification.
example: to brag of one's exploits, courage, or money
exampleZh: 吹噓自己的功績、勇氣或金錢

definition: A fern; bracken.
example: Press the brake.
exampleZh: 踩煞車。

definition: (usually uncountable) Being brave, courageousness.
example: Caesar surpassed all men in bravery.
exampleZh: 凱撒的勇敢超越了所有人。

definition: Able to break or be broken.
example: We had to wrap all the breakables before the movers arrived.
exampleZh: 我們必須在搬運工到達之前把所有易碎物品包起來。

definition: A failure, particularly mechanical; something that has failed
example: We saw a breakdown by the side of the road.
exampleZh: 我們在路邊看到了故障。

definition: The act of breaking up; disintegration or division.
example: David and Amanda announced their breakup.
exampleZh: 大衛和阿曼達宣布分手。

definition: Stunningly beautiful; amazing
example: He went to the Grand Canyon and spent a week taking in the breathtaking scenery all around him.
exampleZh: 他去了大峽谷，花了一周的時間欣賞周圍令人驚嘆的風景。

definition: All animals or plants of the same species or subspecies.
example: a breed of animal
exampleZh: 一種動​​物

definition: With a breeze blowing, with a lively wind, pleasantly windy.
example: Close the window, it's cold and breezy outside.
exampleZh: 關上窗戶，外頭涼風習習。

definition: A building where beer is produced.
example: They couldn't organise a piss-up in a brewery.
exampleZh: 他們不能在啤酒廠裡組織一場小便。

definition: In a brilliant manner; with brilliance.
example: The word "brilliantly" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「brilliantly」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A wide band of electromagnetic frequencies
example: There are big differences in broadband speed and quality from country to country.
exampleZh: 不同國家的寬頻速度和品質有很大差異。

definition: To transmit a message or signal through radio waves or electronic means.
example: The DJ was feeling nervous before his first national broadcast.
exampleZh: DJ 在第一次全國廣播前感到緊張。

definition: An organisation that engages in the activity of broadcasting.
example: NHK is a public broadcaster.
exampleZh: NHK 是一家公共廣播公司。

definition: To make broad or broader.
example: Travel broadens the mind.
exampleZh: 旅行可以開拓視野。

definition: Widely and openly.
example: Vladimir smiled broadly.
exampleZh: 弗拉基米爾笑得燦爛。

definition: A booklet of printed informational matter, like a pamphlet, often for promotional purposes.
example: Leonid found the school brochure.
exampleZh: 列昂尼德找到了學校的小冊子。

definition: A male relative of one's generation, separated by one degree of marriage:
example: My brother-in-law died last Wednesday.
exampleZh: 我的妹夫上週三去世了。

definition: To scan, to casually look through in order to find items of interest, especially without knowledge of what to look for beforehand.
example: You can browse through sentences.
exampleZh: 您可以瀏覽句子。

definition: A person or animal who browses.
example: The browser is not working.
exampleZh: 瀏覽器不工作。

definition: A purplish mark on the skin due to leakage of blood from capillaries under the surface that have been damaged by a blow.
example: Bananas bruise easily.
exampleZh: 香蕉很容易碰傷。

definition: A newly sprouted leaf or blossom that has not yet unfolded.
example: After a long, cold winter, the trees finally began to produce buds.
exampleZh: 經過一個漫長寒冷的冬天，樹木終於開始發芽了。

definition: Of or relating to a budget.
example: We flew on a budget airline.
exampleZh: 我們搭乘廉價航空公司的班機。

definition: A chronic eating disorder characterized by a binge-and-purge cycle - extreme overeating followed by self-induced vomiting.
example: Binging and purging are signs that someone may have bulimia.
exampleZh: 暴食和暴食是某人可能患有貪食症的跡象。

definition: To intimidate (someone) as a bully.
example: You shouldn't bully people for being gay.
exampleZh: 你不應該因為別人是同性戀而欺負他們。

definition: The buttocks.
example: Okay, everyone sit on your bum and try and touch your toes.
exampleZh: 好的，每個人坐在你的屁股上，試著觸摸你的腳趾。

definition: Someone or something that bumps.
example: The company sells screw-on rubber bumpers and feet.
exampleZh: 該公司銷售旋入式橡膠保險桿和腳。

definition: A small bread roll, often sweetened or spiced.
example: He has a bun.
exampleZh: 他有一個髮髻。

definition: To encumber with a literal or figurative burden.
example: to burden a nation with taxes
exampleZh: 給國家帶來稅負

definition: Characteristic of a burden; arduous or demanding
example: When life is so burdensome death has become a sought after refuge.
exampleZh: 當生活如此繁重時，死亡就成了人們尋求的避難所。

definition: Government by bureaus or their administrators or officers.
example: At that time the administration replaced the system of patronage in the civil service with a bureaucracy.
exampleZh: 當時，政府以官僚機構取代了公務員體系中的恩賜制度。

definition: The crime of unlawfully breaking into a vehicle, house, store, or other enclosure with the intent to steal.
example: Ziri was involved in the burglary.
exampleZh: 齊裡參與了這起入室盜竊案。

definition: To commit burglary.
example: Leon was looking to burgle Amelia's house.
exampleZh: 萊昂想要偷竊阿米莉亞的房子。

definition: The act of burying; interment
example: Why do Muslims place such importance on burial?
exampleZh: 為什麼穆斯林如此重視喪葬？

definition: To move busily and energetically with fussiness (often followed by about).
example: The commuters bustled about inside the train station.
exampleZh: 火車站內，乘客們熙熙攘攘。

definition: Busy; full of energy and noisy activity.
example: The commuters bustled about inside the train station.
exampleZh: 火車站內，乘客們熙熙攘攘。

definition: To slaughter (animals) and prepare (meat) for market.
example: The band at that bar really butchered "Hotel California".
exampleZh: 那個酒吧的樂團真是屠殺了《加州旅館》。

definition: A continuous, humming noise, as of bees; a confused murmur, as of general conversation in low tones.
example: Still feeling the buzz from the coffee, he pushed through the last of the homework.
exampleZh: 他仍然感受到咖啡的嗡嗡聲，繼續完成最後一份作業。

definition: A storage closet either separate from, or built into, a wall.
example: Check the medicine cabinet.
exampleZh: 檢查藥櫃。

definition: (material) A long object used to make a physical connection.
example: I tried to watch the movie last night but my cable was out.
exampleZh: 昨晚我想看這部電影，但我的電纜沒電了。

definition: In a calm manner.
example: Talk calmly.
exampleZh: 平靜地說話。

definition: A series of operations undertaken to achieve a set goal.
example: The company is targeting children in their latest advertising campaign.
exampleZh: 該公司在最新的廣告活動中瞄準了兒童。

definition: A person who is running in an election.
example: Which candidate is she backing?
exampleZh: 她支持哪位候選人？

definition: A plant with simple stems, like bamboo or sugar cane, or the stem thereof
example: Mudchester Rovers were caned 10-0.
exampleZh: 穆切斯特流浪者隊以 10-0 慘敗。

definition: (artillery)
example: The first human cannonball was a 14-year-old girl called "Zazel".
exampleZh: 第一個人類砲彈是一位名叫「札澤爾」的14歲女孩。

definition: A generally accepted principle; a rule.
example: The trial must proceed according to the canons of law.
exampleZh: 審判必須按照法律規範進行。

definition: The power or ability to generate an outcome
example: The word "capability" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「能力」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: The descriptive heading or title of a document or part therof
example: Only once the drawing is done will the letterer caption it.
exampleZh: 只有當繪圖完成後，書寫者才會為其添加標題。

definition: One who has been captured or is otherwise confined.
example: captive chains; captive hours
exampleZh: 繫留鏈；圈養時間

definition: A convoy or procession of travelers, their vehicles and cargo, and any pack animals, especially camels crossing a desert.
example: The wedding party got in their cars and caravaned from the chapel to the reception hall.
exampleZh: 婚禮派對上他們的車，從教堂開往接待廳。

definition: The chemical element (symbol C) with an atomic number of 6. It can be found in pure form for example as graphite, a black, shiny and very soft material, or diamond, a colourless, transparent, crystalline solid and the hardest known material.
example: A methane molecule is made up of a single carbon with four hydrogens.
exampleZh: 甲烷分子由一個碳和四個氫組成。

definition: A wood-based material resembling heavy paper, used in the manufacture of boxes, cartons and signs.
example: These cardboard boxes are fragile.
exampleZh: 這些紙板箱很脆弱。

definition: A type of sweater or jumper that fastens up the front with buttons or a zipper, usually machine- or hand-knitted from wool.
example: The advantage of a cardigan is that you don't have to pull it over your head.
exampleZh: 開襟衫的優點是你不必把它拉到頭上。

definition: Without cares or worries; free of concern or worries; without difficulty.
example: He left his cell phone behind and enjoyed a carefree summer day at the beach.
exampleZh: 他放下手機，在海灘享受了無憂無慮的夏日。

definition: (of a person) Kind, sensitive, empathetic.
example: She's a very caring person; she always has a kind word for everyone.
exampleZh: 她是一個非常有愛心的人；她總是對每個人說好話。

definition: Any of a number of festivals held just before the beginning of Lent.
example: Carnival of Brazil
exampleZh: 巴西狂歡節

definition: An arrangement whereby several people travel together in the same car in order to save costs, reduce pollution etc.
example: It makes sense to carpool.
exampleZh: 拼車是有意義的。

definition: To cut.
example: You carve the roast and I'll serve the vegetables.
exampleZh: 你切烤肉，我來端蔬菜。

definition: A waterfall or series of small waterfalls.
example: Mary swishes off her scarf, and waves and waves of black hair cascade down.
exampleZh: 瑪莉甩掉圍巾，一縷縷黑髮如瀑布般垂落下來。

definition: An act of throwing.
example: The area near the stream was covered with little bubbly worm casts.
exampleZh: 靠近溪流的地方佈滿了小泡泡狀的蟲糞。

definition: In a casual manner.
example: Yanni drove away casually.
exampleZh: 雅尼若無其事地開車走了。

definition: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process.
example: Economic development and integration are working as a catalyst for peace.
exampleZh: 經濟發展和一體化正在成為和平的催化劑。

definition: Any large and disastrous event of great significance
example: Experts warned of a catastrophe.
exampleZh: 專家警告可能會發生災難。

definition: Of or pertaining to a catastrophe.
example: The damage could be catastrophic.
exampleZh: 損害可能是災難性的。

definition: Instantly appealing and memorable (of a tune or phrase).
example: a catchy question
exampleZh: 一個吸引人的問題

definition: The business of providing food and related services; foodservice.
example: Benedito's restaurant offers catering services.
exampleZh: Benedito飯店的餐廳提供餐飲服務。

definition: Relating to the throne or the see of a bishop.
example: The cathedral dominates the cityscape.
exampleZh: 大教堂在城市景觀中佔據主導地位。

definition: (often with of, typically of adverse results) The source of, or reason for, an event or action; that which produces or effects a result.
example: They identified a burst pipe as the cause of the flooding.
exampleZh: 他們確定水管爆裂是導致洪水的原因。

definition: To stop.
example: And with that, his twitching ceased.
exampleZh: 就這樣，他的抽搐停止了。

definition: The overhead closure of a room.
example: the dining room had an ornate ceiling
exampleZh: 餐廳有一個華麗的天花板

definition: An enclosed underground space, often under a building, used for storage or shelter.
example: Ziri opened the cellar door.
exampleZh: 齊日打開地窖門。

definition: Someone who plays the cello.
example: Ziri became a cellist.
exampleZh: 齊裡成為大提琴家。

definition: A large stringed instrument of the violin family with four strings, tuned from lowest to highest C-G-D-A, and played with a bow, also possessing an endpin to support the instrument's weight.
example: He plays cello exceptionally well.
exampleZh: 他的大提琴演奏得非常好。

definition: A place where the dead are buried; a graveyard or memorial park.
example: Dmitri discovered a cemetery.
exampleZh: 德米特里發現了一個墓地。

definition: A centigrade temperature scale having the freezing point of water defined as 0° and its boiling point defined as 100° at standard atmospheric pressure. Known as the Celsius scale since 1948.
example: a temperature of 34 centigrades
exampleZh: 氣溫攝氏34度

definition: A document containing a certified statement.
example: The film is certificate 15.
exampleZh: 這部電影的證書是15號。

definition: The act of certifying.
example: The word "certification" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「認證」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: To attest to (a fact) as the truth.
example: These blankets have been certified as fireproof.
exampleZh: 這些毯子已通過防火認證。

definition: To invite (someone) to take part in a competition.
example: We challenged the boys next door to a game of football.
exampleZh: 我們向隔壁的男孩們發起了一場足球比賽。

definition: A senior secretary or official with administrative or legal duties, sometimes in charge of some area of government such as finance or justice.
example: Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster  Lord Chancellor
exampleZh: 蘭開斯特公國大臣  大法官

definition: Filled with chaos.
example: Is life inherently chaotic?
exampleZh: 生活本來就是混亂的嗎？

definition: Being a distinguishing feature of a person or thing.
example: The characteristic of a field, if non-zero, must be a prime number.
exampleZh: 字段的特徵如果非零，則必須是素數。

definition: Pleasant, charismatic.
example: He charmed her with his dashing tales of his days as a sailor.
exampleZh: 他用自己當水手時的精彩故事迷住了她。

definition: To pursue.
example: I need something to chase this shot with.
exampleZh: 我需要一些東西來追逐這張照片。

definition: To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.
example: My brother flunked biology because he cheated on his mid-term.
exampleZh: 我哥哥生物沒及格，因為他期中考作弊。

definition: A point or place where a check is performed, especially a point along a road or on a frontier where travellers are stopped for inspection
example: The travellers were stopped at the checkpoint.
exampleZh: 旅客在檢查站被攔住。

definition: Impudent; impertinent; impertinently bold, often in a way that is regarded as endearing or amusing.
example: Donald started acting cheeky.
exampleZh: 唐納德開始表現得厚顏無恥。

definition: In a cheerful manner.
example: The word "cheerfully" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「愉快地」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: The state of being cheerful; joy.
example: The word "cheerfulness" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「歡樂」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: Any chemical treatment intended to be therapeutic with respect to a disease state.
example: Many cancer patients lose their hair because of chemotherapy.
exampleZh: 許多癌症患者因化療而掉髮。

definition: Highly valued, having a high personal value.
example: I cherish your friendship.
exampleZh: 我珍惜你們的友誼。

definition: A small fruit, usually red, black or yellow, with a smooth hard seed and a short hard stem.
example: I love cherry blossoms.
exampleZh: 我喜歡櫻花。

definition: To crush with the teeth by repeated closing and opening of the jaws; done to food to soften it and break it down by the action of saliva before it is swallowed.
example: Make sure to chew thoroughly, and don't talk with your mouth full!
exampleZh: 一定要充分咀嚼，不要滿嘴說話！

definition: A leader or head of a group of people, organisation, etc.
example: All firefighters report to the fire chief.
exampleZh: 所有消防員均向消防隊長報告。

definition: Of or suitable for a child.
example: Your childish temper tantrums are not going to change my decision on this matter.
exampleZh: 你孩子氣的脾氣不會改變我在這件事上的決定。

definition: A vertical tube or hollow column used to emit environmentally polluting gaseous and solid matter (including but not limited to by-products of burning carbon or hydrocarbon based fuels); a flue.
example: Have you cleaned the chimney?
exampleZh: 你清理煙囪了嗎？

definition: A small piece broken from a larger piece of solid material.
example: This cup has a chip in it.
exampleZh: 這個杯子裡有一個晶片。

definition: To cut into pieces with short, vigorous cutting motions.
example: chop wood; chop an onion
exampleZh: 砍柴；切洋蔥

definition: A part of something that has been separated.
example: The statue broke into chunks.
exampleZh: 雕像碎成碎片。

definition: The act of moving or revolving around, or as in a circle or orbit; a revolution
example: Having circuited the air.
exampleZh: 環繞空氣。

definition: A specific system of electrical circuits in a particular device; the design of such a system.
example: The android maintained an anhydrous core to protect its quantum circuitry.
exampleZh: 機器人保留了一個無水核心來保護其量子電路。

definition: That which attends, or relates to, or in some way affects, a fact or event; an attendant thing or state of things.
example: The word "circumstance" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「情況」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: Throughout a city.
example: They maintained order during the citywide emergency drill.
exampleZh: 他們在全市應急演練期間維持秩序。

definition: A bivalve mollusk of many kinds, especially those that are edible; for example the soft-shell clam (Mya arenaria), the hard clam (Mercenaria mercenaria), the sea clam or hen clam (Spisula solidissima), and other species. The name is said to have been given originally to the Tridacna gigas, a huge East Indian bivalve.
example: Those sneakers cost me fifty clams!
exampleZh: 那雙運動鞋花了我五十蛤！

definition: To strike the palms of the hands together, creating a sharp sound.
example: The children began to clap in time with the music.
exampleZh: 孩子們開始隨著音樂拍手。

definition: (of liquids, such as wine or syrup) To make clear or bright by freeing from feculent matter
example: Leave the wine for 24 hours and it will clarify.
exampleZh: 將酒放置 24 小時，它就會澄清。

definition: A perfect and/or early example of a particular style.
example: Users who dislike the new visual layout can return to classic mode.
exampleZh: 不喜歡新視覺佈局的使用者可以返回經典模式。

definition: The act of forming into a class or classes; a distribution into groups, as classes, orders, families, etc., according to some common relations or attributes.
example: Not every racist country has the same racial classification system.
exampleZh: 並非每個種族主義國家都有相同的種族分類制度。

definition: (grammar) A verb, its necessary grammatical arguments, and any adjuncts affecting them.
example: I have a nudity clause.
exampleZh: 我有一個裸體條款。

definition: Of or relating to clerks or their work.
example: I find it so strange that keyboards can be both musical and clerical.
exampleZh: 我覺得很奇怪，鍵盤可以是音樂鍵盤，也可以是文字鍵盤。

definition: A customer, a buyer or receiver of goods or services.
example: My client is innocent.
exampleZh: 我的當事人是無辜的。

definition: To block or slow passage through (often with 'up').
example: Hair is clogging the drainpipe.
exampleZh: 頭髮堵塞了排水管。

definition: An event or occurrence that signifies an ending.
example: the closure of a door, or of a chink
exampleZh: 門或縫隙的關閉

definition: Any of a wide variety of articles, usually made of fabrics, animal hair, animal skin, or some combination thereof, used to cover the human body for warmth, to preserve modesty, or for fashion.
example: The clothing and unclothing of the idols was of special significance.
exampleZh: 偶像的衣服和脫衣具有特殊的意義。

definition: Awkward, lacking coordination, not graceful, not dextrous.
example: He's very clumsy. I wouldn't trust him with carrying the dishes.
exampleZh: 他非常笨拙。我不相信他會端盤子。

definition: To train.
example: She has coached many opera stars.
exampleZh: 她曾指導過許多歌劇明星。

definition: The process by which someone is coached or tutored; instruction.
example: She has coached many opera stars.
exampleZh: 她曾指導過許多歌劇明星。

definition: A temporary group or union of organizations, usually formed for a particular advantage.
example: The Liberal Democrats and Conservative parties formed a coalition government in 2010.
exampleZh: 2010年，自由民主黨和保守黨組成聯合政府。

definition: Composed of large parts or particles; of inferior quality or appearance; not fine in material or close in texture.
example: coarse language
exampleZh: 粗俗的語言

definition: The shape, outline, or boundary of a coast.
example: Djibouti's coastline is crowded with foreign bases.
exampleZh: 吉布地的海岸線上佈滿了外國基地。

definition: A mixed alcoholic beverage.
example: Scientists found a cocktail of pollutants in the river downstream from the chemical factory.
exampleZh: 科學家在化工廠下游的河流中發現了多種污染物。

definition: The dried and partially fermented fatty seeds of the cacao tree from which chocolate is made.
example: Do you like cocoa?
exampleZh: 你喜歡可可嗎？

definition: A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.
example: Tom drank coconut milk.
exampleZh: 湯姆喝了椰奶。

definition: The quality of cohering, or being coherent; internal consistency.
example: His arguments lacked coherence.
exampleZh: 他的論點缺乏連貫性。

definition: Unified; sticking together; making up a whole.
example: Academic writing should be coherent.
exampleZh: 學術寫作應該連貫。

definition: To occupy exactly the same space.
example: The two squares coincide nicely.
exampleZh: 這兩個正方形很好重合。

definition: Of objects, the property of being coincident; occurring at the same time or place.
example: What a delightful coincidence!
exampleZh: 多麼令人愉快的巧合啊！

definition: To break apart and fall down suddenly; to cave in.
example: Pyramid schemes tend to generate profits for a while and then collapse.
exampleZh: 傳銷往往會產生一段時間的利潤，然後就崩潰了。

definition: A fellow member of a profession, staff, academic faculty or other organization; an associate.
example: Young Fortinbras,/ Holding a weak supposal of our worth/...Colleagued with the dream of his advantage,/...hath not failed to pester us with message/ Importing the surrender of those lands/Lost by his father. - Hamlet (Act I, Scene 2)
exampleZh: 年輕的福丁布拉斯，/對我們的價值抱有微弱的假設/...與他的優勢夢想同行，/...不遺餘力地用信息糾纏我們/進口那些土地的投降/被他父親失去。 - 哈姆雷特（第一幕第二場）

definition: A person who or thing that collects, or which creates or manages a collection.
example: He is an avid collector of nineteenth-century postage stamps.
exampleZh: 他是十九世紀郵票的狂熱收藏家。

definition: The grouping or juxtaposition of things, especially words or sounds.
example: A collocation consists of words that are often found together.
exampleZh: 搭配由經常一起出現的單字組成。

definition: The punctuation mark ":".
example: Tom had colon cancer.
exampleZh: 湯姆患有結腸癌。

definition: Of or pertaining to a colony.
example: The word "colonial" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「殖民地」一詞通常用於正式英語。

definition: A state of unconsciousness from which one may not wake up, usually induced by some form of trauma.
example: Vladimir lapsed into a coma.
exampleZh: 弗拉基米爾陷入昏迷。

definition: (especially of hair or fur) To groom with a toothed implement; chiefly with a comb.
example: I need to comb my hair before we leave the house
exampleZh: 我們離開家前我需要梳理一下頭髮

definition: In a comfortable manner.
example: I snuggled comfortably into the blankets.
exampleZh: 我舒舒服服地躺在毯子裏。

definition: One who exercises control and direction of a military or naval organization.
example: Dmitri is the commander.
exampleZh: 德米特裏是指揮官。

definition: To honour the memory of someone or something with a ceremony or object.
example: On November 11th we commemorate the fallen with a march.
exampleZh: 11 月 11 日，我們舉行遊行紀念陣亡將士。

definition: To remark.
example: I wish I'd commented this complicated algorithm back when I remembered how it worked.
exampleZh: 我希望當我記得這個複雜的演算法是如何運作的時候我就對其進行了評論。

definition: The exchange or buying and selling of commodities; especially the exchange of merchandise, on a large scale, between different places or communities; extended trade or traffic.
example: Many small business owners belong to a chamber of commerce.
exampleZh: 許多小企業主屬於商會。

definition: An advertisement in a common media format, usually radio or television.
example: The word "commercial" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「商業」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A sending or mission (to do or accomplish something).
example: David received his commission after graduating from West Point.
exampleZh: 大衛從西點軍校畢業後獲得了任命。

definition: The well-being of a community.
example: The British Commonwealth of Nations is unable to let go of power.
exampleZh: 英聯邦無法放棄權力。

definition: Eager to communicate; talkative.
example: The word "communicative" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「交際」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A group sharing a common understanding, and often the same language, law, manners, and/or tradition.
example: a community of goods
exampleZh: 商品社區

definition: To exchange substantially; to abate but not abolish completely, a penalty, obligation, or payment in return for a great, single thing or an aggregate; to cash in; to lessen
example: to commute tithes into rentcharges for a sum; to commute market rents for a premium, to commute daily fares for a season ticket
exampleZh: 將什一稅轉換為一筆租金；以溢價換算市場租金，以季票換算每日票價

definition: Deep awareness of the suffering of another, coupled with the wish to relieve it.
example: Compassion should override judgment.
exampleZh: 同情心應該凌駕於判決之上。

definition: Having, feeling or showing compassion (to or toward someone).
example: The Compassionate, the All-Compassionate
exampleZh: 慈悲者，一切慈悲者

definition: Capable of easy interaction.
example: This printer isn't compatible with my computer.
exampleZh: 這台印表機與我的電腦不相容。

definition: To do (something good) after (something bad) happens
example: It is hard work, but they will compensate you well for it.
exampleZh: 雖然工作很辛苦，但他們會給你很好的補償。

definition: The act or principle of compensating.
example: Leonid sought financial compensation.
exampleZh: 列昂尼德尋求經濟賠償。

definition: The quality or state of being competent, i.e. able or suitable for a general role.
example: That question is out with the competence of this court and must be taken to a higher court.
exampleZh: 這個問題不屬於本法院的管轄範圍，必須提交給更高一級的法院。

definition: Having sufficient skill, knowledge, ability, or qualifications.
example: He is a competent skier and an expert snowboarder.
exampleZh: 他是一位出色的滑雪運動員，也是一位滑雪板專家。

definition: To put together; to assemble; to make by gathering things from various sources.
example: Samuel Johnson compiled one of the most influential dictionaries of the English language.
exampleZh: 塞繆爾·約翰遜編撰了最具影響力的英語詞典之一。

definition: The act or state of being or making something complete; conclusion, accomplishment.
example: The word "completion" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「完成」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: The combination of humours making up one's physiological "temperament", being either hot or cold, and moist or dry.
example: a rugged complexion;  a sunburnt complexion
exampleZh: 膚色粗糙；  曬傷的膚色

definition: The state of being complex; intricacy; entanglement.
example: The word "complexity" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「複雜性」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A smaller, self-contained part of a larger entity. Often refers to a manufactured object that is part of a larger device.
example: A CPU is a component of a computer.
exampleZh: CPU 是電腦的一個元件。

definition: Thorough understanding
example: That was beyond my comprehension.
exampleZh: 這超出了我的理解範圍。

definition: Broadly or completely covering; including a large proportion of something.
example: When there are diametrically opposing views on a big issue that concerns millions of people, doing comprehensive research just makes sense.
exampleZh: 當對涉及數百萬人的重大問題有截然相反的觀點時，進行全面的研究才有意義。

definition: To make smaller; to press or squeeze together, or to make something occupy a smaller space or volume.
example: The force required to compress a spring varies linearly with the displacement.
exampleZh: 壓縮彈簧所需的力隨位移線性變化。

definition: An increase in density; the act of compressing, or the state of being compressed; compaction.
example: He has a compression fracture of the spine.
exampleZh: 他患有脊椎壓迫性骨折。

definition: To bind by mutual agreement.
example: He tried to compromise the security in the computer by guessing the password.
exampleZh: 他試圖透過猜測密碼來破壞電腦的安全性。

definition: Required; obligatory; mandatory.
example: The ten-dollar fee was compulsory.
exampleZh: 十美元的費用是強制性的。

definition: To reckon or calculate.
example: Can anyone here compute the square root of 10201?
exampleZh: 這裡有人能算出 10201 的平方根嗎？

definition: To hide something from view or from public knowledge, to try to keep something secret.
example: He tried to conceal the truth about his health.
exampleZh: 他試圖隱瞞有關他健康狀況的真相。

definition: To yield or suffer; to surrender; to grant
example: He conceded the race once it was clear he could not win.
exampleZh: 一旦他明顯無法獲勝，他就認輸了。

definition: Something conceived in the mind; an idea, a thought.
example: a man of quick conceit
exampleZh: 自負的人

definition: Capable of being conceived or imagined
example: It's conceivable.
exampleZh: 這是可以想像的。

definition: To develop an idea; to form in the mind; to plan; to devise; to originate.
example: Assisted procreation can help those trying to conceive.
exampleZh: 輔助生殖可以幫助那些試圖懷孕的人。

definition: The act of conceiving.
example: The word "conception" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「conception」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: To relate or belong to; to have reference to or connection with; to affect the interest of; to be of importance to.
example: A good prince concerns himself in the happiness of his subjects.
exampleZh: 好的君主關心臣民的幸福。

definition: Regarding, respecting.
example: Physically, everything is perfect, but concerning your mental state, you have some problems.
exampleZh: 身體上一切都很完美，但精神狀態上卻存在一些問題。

definition: The act of conceding.
example: Concession stand food is usually overpriced.
exampleZh: 小賣部的食物通常價格過高。

definition: Real, actual, tangible.
example: Fuzzy videotapes and distorted sound recordings are not concrete evidence that bigfoot exists.
exampleZh: 模糊的錄影帶和扭曲的錄音並不是大腳怪存在的具體證據。

definition: To strongly criticise or denounce; to excoriate the perpetrators of.
example: The president condemned the terrorists.
exampleZh: 總統譴責恐怖分子。

definition: To lead, or guide; to escort.
example: The commander conducted thousands of troops.
exampleZh: 統帥率領千軍萬馬。

definition: One who conducts or leads; a guide; a director.
example: train conductor; tram conductor
exampleZh: 列車長；電車售票員

definition: A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
example: A cone is an object (the apex) and a natural transformation from a constant functor (whose image is the apex of the cone and its identity morphism) to a diagram functor. Its components are projections from the apex to the objects of the diagram and it has a “naturality triangle” for each morphism in the diagram. (A “naturality triangle” is just a naturality square which is degenerate at its apex side.)
exampleZh: 圓錐是一個物件（頂點），是從常數函子（其影像是圓錐的頂點及其恆等態射）到圖函子的自然變換。它的組成部分是從頂點到圖的物件的投影，並且對於圖中的每個態射都有一個「自然三角形」。 （「自然三角形」只是一個在其頂點退化的自然正方形。）

definition: The act of consulting together formally; serious conversation or discussion; interchange of views.
example: The conference ended yesterday.
exampleZh: 會議於昨天結束。

definition: To assess (a student) by one-on-one conversation, rather than an examination.
example: The employee and his colleagues were video conferencing on what to do about the challenge.
exampleZh: 這位員工和他的同事正在進行視訊會議，討論如何應對挑戰。

definition: To admit to the truth, particularly in the context of sins or crimes committed.
example: I confess to spray-painting all over that mural!
exampleZh: 我承認那幅壁畫是噴漆的！

definition: The open admittance of having done something (especially something bad).
example: Without the real murderer's confession, an innocent person will go to jail.
exampleZh: 沒有真兇的供述，一個無辜的人就會進監獄。

definition: In a confident manner; with confidence; with strong assurance; positively.
example: Write confidently.
exampleZh: 自信地寫作。

definition: To restrict; to keep within bounds; to shut or keep in a limited space or area.
example: Where did you confine them?
exampleZh: 你把他們關在哪裡了？

definition: To stand or meet facing, especially in competition, hostility or defiance; to come face to face with
example: We should confront him about the missing money.
exampleZh: 我們應該就失去的錢與他對質。

definition: The act of joining, or condition of being joined.
example: Relative pronouns perform as 'conjunction + pronoun'.
exampleZh: 關係代名詞表現為「連接詞+代名詞」。

definition: Victory gained through combat; the subjugation of an enemy.
example: They extended their territory by conquest.
exampleZh: 他們透過征服擴大了領土。

definition: The moral sense of right and wrong, chiefly as it affects one's own behaviour.
example: Your conscience is your highest authority.
exampleZh: 你的良心是你的最高權威。

definition: The state of being conscious or aware; awareness.
example: They regained consciousness.
exampleZh: 他們恢復了知覺。

definition: A process of decision-making that seeks widespread agreement among group members.
example: After years of debate over the best wine to serve at Thanksgiving, no real consensus has emerged.
exampleZh: 經過多年關於感恩節最佳葡萄酒的爭論，尚未達成真正的共識。

definition: Voluntary agreement or permission.
example: After reflecting a little bit, I've consented.
exampleZh: 我稍微想了一下，就答應了。

definition: Significantly; to a degree worth considering.
example: The situation has improved considerably.
exampleZh: 情況已大為改善。

definition: To think about seriously.
example: Consider that we’ve had three major events and the year has hardly begun.
exampleZh: 考慮一下我們已經發生了三件重大事件，而這一年才剛開始。

definition: Of a regularly occurring, dependable nature.
example: He is very consistent in his political choices: economy good or bad, he always votes Labour!
exampleZh: 他的政治選擇非常一致：經濟好壞，他總是投票給工黨！

definition: The act of consoling.
example: Rodrigo needed consolation.
exampleZh: 羅德里戈需要安慰。

definition: A stand-alone cabinet designed to stand on the floor; especially, one that houses home entertainment equipment, such as a TV or stereo system.
example: Console yourself with that.
exampleZh: 以此來安慰自己。

definition: Unchanged through time or space; permanent.
example: constant time   constant space
exampleZh: 恆定時間   恆定空間

definition: To seek the opinion or advice of another; to take counsel; to deliberate together; to confer.
example: Consult your specialist.
exampleZh: 請諮詢您的專家。

definition: A person or party that is consulted
example: The word "consultant" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「顧問」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: To make something dangerous or toxic by introducing impurities or foreign matter.
example: This water is contaminated. It isn't safe to drink.
exampleZh: 這些水被污染了。飲用並不安全。

definition: From the same time period, coexistent in time.
example: Cervantes was a contemporary of Shakespeare.
exampleZh: 塞萬提斯是莎士比亞的同時代人。

definition: The state or act of contemning; the feeling or attitude of regarding someone or something as inferior, base, or worthless; scorn, disdain.
example: Antonio showed nothing but contempt.
exampleZh: 安東尼奧除了蔑視之外什麼也沒表現出來。

definition: Satisfied
example: You can't have any more - you'll have to content yourself with what you already have.
exampleZh: 你不能再擁有更多了——你必須滿足於你已經擁有的。

definition: An agreement between two or more parties, to perform a specific job or work order, often temporary or of fixed duration and usually governed by a written agreement.
example: Marriage is a contract.
exampleZh: 婚姻是一份契約。

definition: To deny the truth of (a statement or statements).
example: His testimony contradicts hers.
exampleZh: 他的證詞與她的證詞相矛盾。

definition: The act of contradicting.
example: His contradiction of the proposal was very interesting.
exampleZh: 他對這個提議的反駁非常有趣。

definition: That contradicts something, such as an argument.
example: Tom's contradictory explanations only befogged the issue further.
exampleZh: 湯姆自相矛盾的解釋只會讓問題更加撲朔迷離。

definition: A debate or discussion of opposing opinions; (generally) strife.
example: This controversy is futile!
exampleZh: 這種爭論是沒有意義的！

definition: In a convenient manner, form, or situation; without difficulty.
example: The word "conveniently" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「方便」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: A meeting or gathering.
example: The convention was held in Geneva.
exampleZh: 大會在日內瓦舉行。

definition: Pertaining to a convention, as in following generally accepted principles, methods and behaviour.
example: They wear conventional clothes, eat conventional food, and keep conventional hours.
exampleZh: 他們穿傳統的衣服，吃傳統的食物，做傳統的工作時間。

definition: Free verbal interchange of thoughts or views; conversation; chat.
example: The word "converse" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「converse」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: With a reversed relationship.
example: The word "conversely" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「相反」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: The act of converting something or someone.
example: His conversion to Christianity
exampleZh: 他皈依基督教

definition: To transform or change (something) into another form, substance, state, or product.
example: A kettle converts water into steam.
exampleZh: 水壺將水轉化為蒸氣。

definition: In a state of believing, especially from evidence but not necessarily.
example: He was convinced he was a great singer, statements of others to the contrary.
exampleZh: 他堅信自己是一位偉大的歌手，但其他人的說法恰恰相反。

definition: A moderate or refreshing state of cold; moderate temperature of the air between hot and cold; coolness.
example: in the cool of the morning
exampleZh: 在涼爽的早晨

definition: To work or act together, especially for a common purpose or benefit.
example: You should cooperate.
exampleZh: 你應該合作。

definition: (usually uncountable) The act of cooperating.
example: The word "cooperation" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「合作」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: Ready to work with another person or in a team; ready to cooperate.
example: The patient was rarely cooperative, and tended to refuse treatment.
exampleZh: 患者很少合作，並傾向於拒絕治療。

definition: To deal effectively with something, especially if difficult.
example: I thought I would never be able to cope with life after the amputation, but I have learned how to be happy again.
exampleZh: 我以為我將永遠無法應付截肢後的生活，但我已經學會如何再次快樂。

definition: A reddish-brown, malleable, ductile metallic element with high electrical and thermal conductivity, symbol Cu, and atomic number 29.
example: Copper conducts electricity well.
exampleZh: 銅的導電性能良好。

definition: A hard substance made of the limestone skeletons of marine polyps.
example: Algerian coasts are illegally plundered for their coral.
exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞海岸的珊瑚遭到非法掠奪。

definition: The central part of fruit, containing the kernels or seeds.
example: the core of an apple or quince
exampleZh: 蘋果或柑橘的核心

definition: A stone forming the base at the corner of a building.
example: The cornerstone on the Flatiron Building is set on the Fifth Avenue facade.
exampleZh: 熨斗大廈的基石位於第五大道的正面。

definition: A musical instrument of the brass family, slightly smaller than a trumpet, usually in the musical key of B-flat.
example: A cornet looks similar to a trumpet, but it has a mellower tone.
exampleZh: 短號看起來與小號相似，但音色較柔和。

definition: The luminous plasma atmosphere of the Sun or other star, extending millions of kilometres into space, most easily seen during a total solar eclipse.
example: Biotech and Moderna have a lead in the Corona or Covid vaccination.
exampleZh: Biotech 和 Moderna 在新冠疫苗接種方面處於領先地位。

definition: Having a physical, tangible body; material, corporeal.
example: corporal punishment
exampleZh: 體罰

definition: Of or relating to a corporation.
example: The one on Seventh Street is a corporate franchise.
exampleZh: 第七街的那家是一家企業特許經營店。

definition: A body corporate, created by law or under authority of law, having a continuous existence independent of the existences of its members, and powers and liabilities distinct from those of its members.
example: Damiano is the corporation scientist.
exampleZh: 達米亞諾是公司科學家。

definition: Free from error; true; accurate.
example: That is correct!
exampleZh: 這是正確的！

definition: Freedom from error
example: The word "correctness" is commonly used in formal English.
exampleZh: 「正確性」一詞在正式英語中常用。

definition: (constructed with to) To be equivalent or similar in character, quantity, quality, origin, structure, function etc.
example: I've been corresponding with my German pen pal for three years.
exampleZh: 我和我的德國筆友已經通信三年了。

definition: Friendly discussion.
example: Correspondence conducted in Volapük.
exampleZh: 通信在沃拉普克進行。

definition: Someone who or something which corresponds.
example: Skura was a reluctant correspondent.
exampleZh: 斯庫拉是一位不情願的記者。

definition: That have a similar relationship
example: I've been corresponding with my German pen pal for three years.
exampleZh: 我和我的德國筆友已經通信三年了。

definition: A narrow hall or passage with rooms leading off it, as in a building or in a railway carriage.
example: He disappeared into the corridor.
exampleZh: 他消失在走廊上。
